heroHeadline: Irrawaddy: 100+ Civilians Killed Since Junta 'Civilian Rule' Began; SAC Deploys ~10 Jets in Falam; Drone Bombs School in Sagaing; Resistance Captures Outpost Near Naypyidaw
heroSubtitle: On April 24, 2026 — Day 1,908 of Myanmar's post-coup civil war — the Irrawaddy documented that the SAC has killed more than 100 civilians through airstrikes since the junta parliament convened March 16, exposing the 'civilian rule' transition as a political facade accompanied by intensified aerial violence. April 23 saw the SAC deploy approximately 10 fighter jets in a major assault on Falam Township, Chin State (retaliation for a resistance drone strike that killed 2 soldiers on April 22), while a separate SAC Infantry Battalion No. 42 suicide drone bombed a village school in Wetlet Township, Sagaing, injuring 11 — including 10 students and a teacher. SAC jets also bombed Ngapali tourist town (Thandwe Township, Rakhine State) at 3:00 PM. On April 22, resistance forces captured a junta outpost in Lewe Township, Naypyidaw Union Territory, killing 10 soldiers including a major in one of the closest attacks to the SAC capital since 2021 — while SAC jets separately killed 4 young civilians (ages 15-20) fishing near Letpadan, Bago, and dropped ~50 bombs on Paletwa Township, Chin State. MoeMaKa's concurrent analysis confirmed the SAC has now conducted 9,400+ total airstrikes since the coup with 3,300+ in 2025-2026 alone. Canada reaffirmed recognition of U Win Myint as Myanmar's legitimate president on April 23 as international isolation of Min Aung Hlaing's junta government deepened.
footerNote: Data sourced from AAPP, ACLED, UN OCHA, Irrawaddy, RFA, Myanmar Now, DVB, Al Jazeera, Mizzima, Human Rights Watch, Crisis Group, Myanmar Peace Monitor, MoeMaKa CDM News, Japan Times, Khaosod English, The Nation Thailand, Foreign Policy, Xinhua, BERNAMA, Anadolu Agency, IMNA/Mon News, FDD, and official NUG, KNU, KIA, and AA statements.
